Should attend all courses from first week.

Question: "I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?"
Analyze the Evidence:**

[1] Discusses "Kenkyusei" (research students) eligibility. It states coursework does *not* count toward a degree for Kenkyusei. It mentions transferring students must follow a screening process and coursework completed as Kenkyusei will not be applied towards ICU's residency requirement. This is specific to Kenkyusei status, not general study abroad or major planning for regular students.

[2] Discusses Admissions Requirements by Country/Region. Mentions submitting documents verifying completion of secondary education. For Transfer applicants, documents are exempted if they have a bachelor's degree. Mentions submitting a list of courses progressing or to be taken in the final semester/year to verify course requirements for admission.

[3] FD Newsletter Vol. 29. Mentions APS (Admissions/Student Services?) presents advice for course planning before studying abroad. Directs students to watch recordings/materials from previous sessions. Recommends consulting **IBS advisors** for scheduling. Mentions APS staff review the Graduation Requirement Checklist on **icuMAP** for graduation requirement concerns

Question: If I take a leave of absence and later return to ICU, what procedure or office information should I check before planning my courses again?
Analyze the Evidence:**
[1] Title: ICU Health Care Office for Students - 感染症が疑われた時の流れ (Infectious disease flow).
Content: Discusses informing class teachers, faculty contact info, faculty asking for doctor's certificate/proof for sick leave. Healthcare Office does not issue certificates for absences.
Relevance: Mentions sick leave/absences and faculty contact, but focuses on infectious disease and proof requirements for leave.
[2] Title: ehandbook - Ryugaku, Leave of Absence, Reenrolment, Withdrawal, Readmission, Status Extension, Status Change, Dismissal.
Content: Specifically mentions "Ryugaku, Leave of Absence, Reenrolment".

Key Info: Upon return, students must submit the "Report of Return from Ryugaku" to the EAG (Exchange Abroad Group? or similar office) before Registration day. Failure results in inability to register. If requesting transfer credits, submit catalog/bulletin/syllabi of courses taken abroad.

Question: "I am comparing dormitory options and also thinking about whether scholarships or fees affect my budget. Where should I check housing and cost-related information?"
Analyze the Evidence:**

[1] Title: Housing Office. Source: http://office.icu.ac.jp/ho/en. Evidence lists links/topics like "Dormitory Screening Results", "Common Rules", "Outline & Dormitory Fees", "Regulations and Bylaws", "FAQ", "Emergency Contact Information". This suggests the Housing Office website is the primary source for dormitory fees and rules.

[2] Title: Researchers Information System. Evidence mentions "Dormitory Application and Details", "Housing Office Website", "Off-campus Housing", "ICU Service Co., Ltd. Website", "Homestay". This provides a list of housing options (on-campus, off-campus, homestay) and points to the Housing Office website.

[3] Title: 【APPLY BY4/10】2026 Summer ★International Summer Program in Japan (ISPJ). Evidence mentions "Dormitory fees will be charged" and "You will be informed by the Housing Office once your participation is confirmed". This confirms fees exist but doesn't give a general budgeting source other than the Housing Office.

Question: "I feel overwhelmed and want to talk to someone, but I am worried about privacy. Where can I find official information about counseling or student support at ICU?"

IBS is official peer advisers.
Start of advising sessions for new students.
Topics: JLP classes, course selection, registration.
Not specifically about mental health counseling or privacy, but peer advising.

Question: "If I need exam accommodations because of a disability or health condition, where should I start, and what kind of official support page should I look for?"

[1] English Linguistics Syllabus: Mentions "Learning Accessibility Services" and "course instructor". Says consult with them. Mentions "Basic Policy for Supporting Students with Disabilities".

[2] Student Guide (CTL): Details the process. Step 1: Meet with Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) staff. Step 2: Submit documents (medical cert, advisor signature, etc.). Mentions "exam accommodations" (e.g., extended time, separate room).
[3] Accounting Syllabus: Similar to [1]. Mentions "Learning Accessibility Services" and "course instructor".
[4] For Students | CTL English: Details the process in English. Step 1: Meet with LAS staff. Step 2: Submit documents.
Synthesize the Answer:**
Where to start?* Consult with the course instructor and the Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) [1], [3]. For exam accommodations specifically,

Question: "I am graduating from undergraduate school but continuing to ICU graduate school. What should I know about my IT account or services after graduation?"
Analyze the Evidence:**

[1] "IT Center For ALL Users - To Students Graduating": Mentions accounts (ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, Google Workspace, etc.) expire on March 31, 2026. Recommends using a personal email for post-graduation email exchange. Mentions email forwarding.

[2] "To Students Graduating in March 2026": Reiterates accounts expire March 31, 2026. Recommends personal email for post-graduation. States email forwarding stops. Advises backing up files (Gmail/Google Drive). Lists consequences of account deletion (can't log in to Google, miss announcements, lose graduate school files).
[3] "Paths after Graduation｜ICU": Mentions 20% pursue further education. Mentions 5-year Program. No specific IT details.
[4] "Graduate School Overview｜ICU": Mentions Graduate School Overview. No specific IT details.
Synthesize the Answer:**
The user is graduating undergrad but continuing grad school.
Key IT info: Account expiration

Question: "I transferred credits before entering ICU. Can the system tell me exactly how many more credits I personally need to graduate early?"
Analyze the Evidence:**

[1] "Information on Transfer of Credits (for Transfer Student)｜ICU": Mentions courses eligible for transfer (equivalent, approved towards graduation, grades >= 70). States credit transfers are granted only *after* entering ICU.
[2] "Application Documents｜ICU": Mentions submitting transcripts. States "Transfer credits will be granted only when students complete the credit transfer procedure after entering ICU."
[3] "Application Documents｜ICU": Similar to [2], repeats grading system requirements and credit transfer timing.
[4] "Graduate School - Credit Transfer": Discusses Graduate School credit transfer (Master's course requirement, up to 15 credits). Mentions partner schools and exchange agreements.
Evaluate the Question against Evidence:**
Question asks: Can the system tell me exactly how many more credits I personally need to graduate early?
Evidence [1]: States that credit transfer happens *after* entering ICU and depends on course equivalence and grades. It does not mention a system that calculates remaining credits

Question: If the chatbot answer and the ICU Portal announcement seem different about a deadline, which source should I trust and what should I do next?
Analyze the Evidence:**

[1] & [2]: ICU Portal Manuals. Content focuses on checking the portal daily, searching notices via "ALL"/"MORE", email distribution categories ("公示", "重要なお知らせ"), push notifications, and permissions for email distribution. It mentions checking the portal daily. It does not explicitly address conflict resolution between chatbot and portal announcements.
[3]: ehandbook - November 28, 2023. Mentions thesis submission deadlines, graduation list announcement dates, and unpaid fees deadlines. It provides contact info (ea-group@icu.ac.jp).
[4]: To Students Graduating in March 2026. Mentions account expiration dates and backup recommendations.
Evaluate Evidence against Question:**
The question asks about trust hierarchy when there is a discrepancy between a "chatbot answer" and an "ICU Portal announcement" regarding a deadline.
Evidence [1] and [2] (ICU Portal Manuals) state that students should check the ICU Portal announcements daily. They mention checking the "ALL"/"MORE" link box
